notes.anchor: TVLLIVS ET CICERO ET Q. Q. TIRONI S. P. D.
notes.note: The senders are Cicero ('Tullius'), his son young Marcus ('Cicero'), and the two Quintuses ('Q. Q.', the brother Quintus and his son the nephew); 'S. P. D.' = salutem plurimam dicit, 'sends warmest greetings.'

Patrae (modern Patras) on the north coast of the Peloponnese, where Tiro had been left behind ill in the household of the banker Curius; Cicero routes letters to him through every traveller heading that way.

notes.anchor: The seventh day before the Ides of November
notes.note: 7 November 50 BC. Roman dates count inclusively backward from the Ides (the 13th in November), so 'the seventh day before' is the 7th.
A harbour at the mouth of the Ambracian Gulf in western Greece, a standard waypoint on the sea route home to Italy; Cicero is on his way back from his Cilician command.
